name: USS Kittiwake Wreck
location: Seven Mile Beach, Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands
description: The USS Kittiwake (ASR-13) is a 251-foot submarine rescue vessel sunk as an artificial reef on January 5, 2011. Resting in 20m of water off Seven Mile Beach, it is one of the Caribbean's premier wreck dives. The wreck is fully penetrable with large openings, swim-throughs, and multiple decks accessible. Hurricane Nate shifted the wreck onto its starboard side in 2017, creating a unique sideways wreck experience.

seasonalNotes.jan-apr: Calm seas, best visibility (30-40m). Humpback whale sightings possible offshore. Water temp 26-27°C.
seasonalNotes.may-jun: Start of summer, water warming to 28-29°C. Good conditions. Turtle nesting season begins on Seven Mile Beach.
seasonalNotes.jul-oct: Hurricane season. Generally calm between storms. Warmest water 29-30°C. Lionfish most active. Best time for spotted eagle ray sightings.
seasonalNotes.nov-dec: Post-hurricane season. Occasional northerly swells can reduce visibility. Water cooling to 27-28°C. Nassau grouper spawning aggregation (protected).

diveSites.name: Kittiwake — Full Circuit
diveSites.description: Descend to sand at 20m, circle the hull, then work up through the wreck. Exit at the bridge at 5m for safety stop with a view.

diveSites.name: Kittiwake — Engine Room Penetration
diveSites.description: Enter through the main hatch amidships, navigate through the engine room. Large open spaces with excellent light penetration. Watch for nurse sharks resting inside.

diveSites.name: Kittiwake — Top Deck & Snorkel
diveSites.description: Shallow area over the top deck and mast. Accessible to snorkelers. Schools of sergeant majors, blue tang, and juvenile fish use the wreck as shelter.
